🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Oxwich Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* south wales
*County:* Swansea
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 51.55463, -4.16791
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxwich_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Oxwich%20Castle/@51.554,-4.148,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Oxwich_Castle_4.jpg/330px-Oxwich_Castle_4.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Oxwich Castle is a Grade I listed castle which occupies a position on a wooded headland overlooking Oxwich Bay on the Gower Peninsula, Wales. Although it may occupy the site of an earlier fortification, it is a castle in name only as it is a grand Tudor fortified manor house built in courtyard style.

_Listing 3 of 33_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Laugharne Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* west wales
*County:* Carmarthenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 51.76972222, -4.46194444
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laugharne_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Laugharne%20Castle/@51.7712,-4.4625,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNrU9wWuKAxdoItElJfQWvYC8AORegBSbzij2eqtAlr24za4KNpuQvUZZwFi5JCisjci_31pqefYoneVl2dFBV4d5Dc-il81T-J8u27jtaIs2GGrXcueLg1QWC_QrNom2ALXiQtmcwWpmBRUw=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Laugharne Castle is in Laugharne, Carmarthenshire, Wales. The castle, located on the estuary of the River Tâf, was originally established in 1116. It was rebuilt as a Norman stronghold. There have been many alterations since then, including becoming a Tudor fortified manor house in the sixteenth century. It changed hands twice during the English Civil War, being eventually captured by Parliamentary forces in 1644.

_Listing 5 of 33_

listing.name: Laugharne Castle
listing.desc: Laugharne Castle is in Laugharne, Carmarthenshire, Wales. The castle, located on the estuary of the River Tâf, was originally established in 1116. It was rebuilt as a Norman stronghold. There have been many alterations since then, including becoming a Tudor fortified manor house in the sixteenth century. It changed hands twice during the English Civil War, being eventually captured by Parliamentary forces in 1644.

imageAttribution.description: Dylan admires ....... the medieval Laugharne castle. The foreground shows the rear view of the wooden sculpture of Dylan Thomas set in the Millennium Garden. The engraved detail on the front of the base is shown at the link: 1708269
